what_it_does: Defines a connection contract that specifies how frontend surfaces address, send commands to, and read state and context from the backend and corpus, using canonical ui:// and run:// address grammars and a unified per-address record.
observations: Introduces two address schemes: ui://<region>/<element>[/<sub>][/@state] for UI surfaces and run://<graph>/<node> for live graph-node instances.
Defines a union per-address record with fields kind, region, title, represents, code, capabilities as a bool-object, resolve via domHandle or cameraRef, states, and tier.
Tags every clause as IS (built and reachable now) or SHOULD-BE (Phase-2 target not yet built).
Proposes a future address-keyed command seam POST /api/act with verb + address + args, while warning not to call it today.
Lists a 7-verb whitelist: run, propose, build, consult, show, panel, extend; apply/delete/file-write are unreachable from the agent face.
States that a click indicates and consents but does not execute; governance rides the address tier, not the verb.
Requires state rendering to be read from served node_states, never hardcoded client-side.
Proposes a future context endpoint GET /api/context?address=<ui://...> for address-keyed auto-resolve.
Requires DOM elements to declare full ui:// addresses in data-ui-ref and canvas nodes to declare run:// addresses.
Ends with a reconciliation table comparing corpus, live app, and canonical positions on grammar, capabilities, kind, command, state read, context read, and governance.
standouts: The pervasive IS/SHOULD-BE honesty tagging, making it explicit which seams are live and which are still targets.
The governance principle that a click indicates/consents but does not execute, and that governance is resolved by address tier rather than verb.
The reconciliation of corpus string-list capabilities with live bool-object capabilities into a canonical bool-object using drivenReadOnly.
The future /api/act address-keyed command seam and /api/context?address=... context seam.
conventions: Uses numbered sections and subsections like an RFC or specification.
Tags clauses with IS or SHOULD-BE to distinguish current implementation from planned implementation.
Uses JSONc blocks for record and command examples.
Maintains a three-way comparison among corpus, live app, and canonical target.
References external files and completion criteria rather than defining them inline.
concerns: A large portion of the contract is SHOULD-BE, which could be mistaken for live behavior despite the warnings.
The capabilities vocabulary mismatch (driven / driven-read-only vs drivenReadOnly) signals existing drift that the contract must reconcile.
Governance by address tier is not yet implemented but is described as the future gate for mutating commands.
The context endpoint and address-keyed command seam are described as net-new with no schema or error semantics beyond the examples.
notes: A design contract that maps UI addresses to backend commands and state, carefully separating what already works from what is still Phase-2.
questions: What process assigns the tier value (AUTO/SURFACE/CONFIRM/LOCKED) to each address?
How are orphan addresses handled at runtime when data-ui-ref points to an unregistered address?
What is the exact request/response schema and error handling for the proposed /api/act endpoint?
How does the SSE stream stamp events with ui:// addresses in meta, and how does the FE filter them?
What determines whether a canvas node resolves via cameraRef versus domHandle?
How is the operator's current locus persisted and read by the RHM?
purpose_vs_actual: Yes — the file states it is an importable spec for the blueprint builder describing how a surface talks to the running system, and it delivers a grammar, per-address record, command/state/context flows, and a reconciliation table.
apparent_themes: Address-centric identity for UI elements and running nodes.
Explicit honesty about live versus planned implementation.
Governance resolved by address posture, not by verb or scheme alone.
Corpus-live-canonical convergence through a single grammar.
intention_signals: To establish one address grammar and per-address record that the frontend, backend, and corpus can all share, while clearly marking which parts are already built and which are Phase-2 targets.
novelty: The IS/SHOULD-BE tagging throughout a design contract, turning it into a build spec that warns implementers not to treat future seams as present.
contribution: A shared address grammar and per-address record schema that name UI elements and running nodes, describe their capabilities and governance posture, and define how surfaces send commands and read state and context.
summary_for_embedding: company/design/CONNECTION-CONTRACT.md is a design contract that defines a canonical ui:// and run:// address grammar, a unified per-address record, address-keyed command and context seams, and a three-way reconciliation of corpus, live app, and canonical frontend/backend behavior.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to give the blueprint builder a single importable spec for how frontend surfaces address the running system and to align the corpus, live app, and future implementation around one address grammar.
